panels.title: Command Latency p99 (committed)
panels.description: p99 end-to-end latency per command, measured at the application-service boundary outside @Transactional (commit time is included). Filtered to outcome=committed so happy-path latency isn't muddied by idempotent replays or error fast-paths.

panels.title: Concurrency Conflict Ratio (per command)
panels.description: Fraction of commands that fail with optimistic-locking / transaction-id conflicts (concurrency_conflict ÷ total). Ratio is the meaningful SLI here — absolute rate scales with traffic, so 0.1/s at 1 RPS is a fire while 0.1/s at 1000 RPS is noise. Sustained > a few % per command signals account-level contention (hot account, retrying clients, or racing producers). Note: under zero traffic the expression is 0/0 = NaN and renders as a gap; that's expected. Reusing this expression for paging requires `or vector(0)` to guard against NaN.

targets.expr: sum by (command) (rate(wealthpay_account_command_seconds_count{job="wealthpay", instance=~"$instance", outcome="concurrency_conflict"}[5m])) / sum by (command) (rate(wealthpay_account_command_seconds_count{job="wealthpay", instance=~"$instance"}[5m]))

panels.title: Account Load Duration
panels.description: p95/p99 latency for rehydrating an account from the event store — growth signals snapshotting misfunction

panels.title: Account Load Rate
panels.description: Throughput of account rehydrations — correlate with HTTP request rate

panels.title: Event Store Append Latency p99 (per outcome)
panels.description: p99 latency of the critical-path write to event_store, broken down by outcome (success vs optimistic_conflict). Conflicts are typically much faster than successful appends (no INSERT, just a version check rejection) — a sustained convergence of conflict and success p99 suggests contention is dominating the success path's tail.

panels.title: Snapshot Save Latency p99 (per outcome)
panels.description: p99 latency of snapshot persistence to the account_snapshot table, broken down by outcome. Snapshot saves are best-effort (failures are caught by the application service and never block the critical path) — a sustained non-zero rate of failure outcomes signals a real DB issue worth investigating. Note: snapshots only fire every ACCOUNT_SNAPSHOT_THRESHOLD events per account (default 100). At low traffic this panel can read empty/NaN — that's no signal, not no problem. Pair with the rate panel below to disambiguate.

panels.title: Event Store Append Rate (per outcome)
panels.description: Throughput of event_store appends, broken down by outcome (success vs optimistic_conflict). Pair with the latency panel above — non-zero rate validates that latency p99 reflects real traffic. Conflict rate spikes here will also show on the per-command Concurrency Conflict Ratio panel.

panels.title: Snapshot Save Rate (per outcome)
panels.description: Throughput of snapshot saves, broken down by outcome. Disambiguates the latency panel: an empty rate panel + empty latency panel together = no recent saves (expected at low traffic given snapshots only fire every ACCOUNT_SNAPSHOT_THRESHOLD events). A non-zero failure rate is page-worthy regardless of latency — saves are best-effort, so failures don't surface as 5xx but do indicate snapshotting is broken (and aggregates will rehydrate slower over time).
